title: Bound a whole-file JSON transformation's memory use
prompt: JsonStreamTransformer currently reads and materializes an entire JSON array before transforming it. Replace that whole-document path with incremental, bounded-memory processing. Keep the public constructor and transform(input, output) API, and use the injected buffer_tracker's observe(byte_count) method to report the live input buffer. Peak observed input buffering must stay bounded as the number of small records grows (the protected suite enforces a 4096-byte ceiling for 20,000 records). Preserve array order and the existing per-record transformation, including replacement of an existing sequence key. For every accepted input, emit exactly the same compact JSON bytes as the current implementation, including preserving its whole-document nesting limit. Preserve InvalidDocument and InvalidRecord classes/messages, including document validation taking precedence over record validation: malformed JSON is InvalidDocument even if an earlier array element is not an object. A malformed document, a non-array document, or an invalid record must leave output untouched, so stage output with bounded memory if necessary. Handle JSON strings, escapes, UTF-8 Unicode, nested objects/arrays, primitives, leading/trailing whitespace, and chunk boundaries correctly. Use only the Ruby standard library; tests and the implementation must run offline and deterministically.
